prose:
Method provenance:
Property: atom‐condensed Fukui functions (f⁺ for electrophilicity, f⁻ for nucleophilicity), dual descriptor, and identification of the most electrophilic/nucleophilic sites in phenol.
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2‐TZVP (tier `standard`), no density fitting (exact four-center integrals) in PySCF 2.x.
Geometry provenance: fixed geometry taken as–is from the supplied file `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/phenol/phenol.xyz`; no further optimisation was performed (single-point population analyses on this structure).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Charge & multiplicity: charge 0 (neutral), multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet). N−1 (cation) and N+1 (anion) states were run as doublets (multiplicity 2) for the finite-difference Fukui derivatives.
Software/backend: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill → PySCF backend (DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP) for the three single-point calculations.
Convergence & warnings: all three SCF calculations converged; integrity check reports status "ok" and trustworthy true.  Warnings returned by chemkit (verbatim) are reproduced below.

Live log: the PySCF stdout/stderr was streamed in real time to a file created by chemkit (e.g. `fukui_YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.out`) in the working directory while the run was in progress; you can inspect it for full SCF details.

Headline numerical highlight (most electrophilic site):
